headline: Duke Energy (DUK) Launches AI Bill Insights For Carolinas Customers
summary: Duke Energy has introduced an AI-powered Bill Insights feature in its mobile app for customers in the Carolinas. The tool provides personalized, plain-language explanations of monthly energy bills, with a focus on higher summer usage. The rollout reflects a move toward more digital, data driven customer support in the utility sector. Duke Energy, traded as NYSE:DUK, is adding this AI feature at a time when its stock is priced at $128.33 and has returned 9.3% year to date and 12.6% over the...

headline: Duke Energy's nearly $1 billion investment with North Carolina suppliers strengthens American supply chains, local economies and the nation's energy future
summary: As the nation approaches Independence Day, Duke Energy is investing in American suppliers to help power local economies, support customer value and build the energy infrastructure needed to serve growing communities.

headline: CEG or DUK: Which Stock Deserves to Be in Your Portfolio Now?
summary: DUK edges CEG on earnings estimates, dividend yield, capital plans, valuation and six-month share gains as clean power demand rises.

headline: Take Home an Electrician’s Paycheck Without the High Voltage
summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics puts the median electrician at roughly $62,000 a year, while many experienced electricians earn $65,000 to $80,000 or more once overtime enters the picture. Replacing that paycheck with dividend income is the question this article answers. The math is straightforward: income target divided by yield equals capital required. For simplicity, ... headline: Duke gives up offshore wind lease for ‘partial’ reimbursement under Interior deal
summary: A Duke Energy subsidiary paid $155 million for the lease in 2022, saying it could support up to 1.6 GW of capacity. The utility now says it will “refocus” nearly $129 million to projects that may include new nuclear, natural gas and grid enhancements.
